titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Little Square Review Archive
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 30, 2018 at 06:59
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Little Square Review Records contain correspondence, printed material, production files, a photograph reproduction, proofs, and business papers relating to the production and publication of journal issues.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ralph B. Sipper, Bookseller on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2000. Gift of John Ridland, 2000-2005.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Little Square Review Records are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Little Square Review Records. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
The collection received preliminary processing at the time of acquisition. Further organization, rehousing, and description were carried out in 2013.

The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as: Uncat MSS 28, Uncat MSS 492, and Uncat MSS 808. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during basic processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Little Square Review
bioghist.p: The Little Square Review , edited by poet John Ridland, was published from 1966 to 1972. Each issue of The Little Square Review was devoted to the work of one or two poets that Ridland considered to have not received sufficient recognition in larger publications. Ten issues were released during its existence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Little Square Review Records contain correspondence, printed material, production files, a photograph reproduction, proofs, and business papers relating to the production and publication of journal issues.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: I. April 2000 Acquisition, 1960-2000. II. April 2003 Acquisition, 1974. III. August 2005 Acquisition, undated.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: Office Files, Correspondence, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, printed material, production files, proofs and business papers relating to the production of each issue.

did.unittitle: Little Square Review #5 and #6. (Indian issue with N. H. Russell)
Little Square Review #7. Three Western Poets. Robert Brandts, F. R. Bresgal, Dick Strong
Little Square Review #8. Two Northwest Poets
Ignotow, David and Rose Graublatt. Chelsea submissions and acceptances
Reproduction of photograph of Ishi, inscribed on verso by William Stafford to John Ridland
Skinner, John, Version of American Indian texts, typescripts, various drafts
Box of index cards containing subscription and sales information
